notes: Fixture entry manually transcribed from the extraction sample and source PDF.

text.original: Play the Troubadour if you want to play music to bolster your allies.
text.summary: A Bard subclass focused on songs that support allies.

foundation.name: Gifted Performer
foundation.text: You can play three different types of songs, once each per long rest; describe how you perform for others to gain the listed benefit: Relaxing Song clears a Hit Point for you and all allies within Close range. Epic Song makes a target within Close range temporarily Vulnerable. Heartbreaking Song gives you and all allies within Close range a Hope.

specialization.name: Maestro
specialization.text: Your rallying songs steel the courage of those who listen. When you give a Rally Die to an ally, they can gain a Hope or clear a Stress.

mastery.name: Virtuoso
mastery.text: You are among the greatest of your craft and your skill is boundless. You can perform each of your Gifted Performer feature's songs twice per long rest.

notes: Fixture entry manually transcribed from the extraction sample and source PDF.

text.original: Play the Wordsmith if you want to use clever wordplay and captivate crowds.
text.summary: A Bard subclass focused on speeches, wordplay, and social support.

foundation.name: Rousing Speech
foundation.text: Once per long rest, you can give a heartfelt, inspiring speech. All allies within Far range clear 2 Stress.

foundation.name: Heart of a Poet
foundation.text: After you make an action roll to impress, persuade, or offend someone, you can spend a Hope to add a d4 to the roll.

specialization.name: Eloquent
specialization.text: Once per session, when you encourage an ally, you can allow them to find a mundane object or tool they need, help an Ally without spending Hope, or give them an additional downtime move during their next rest.

mastery.name: Epic Poetry
mastery.text: Your Rally Die increases to a d10. Additionally, when you Help an Ally, you can narrate the moment as if you were writing the tale of their heroism in a memoir. When you do, roll a d10 as your advantage die.
